_notes: Chunk is entirely sanctoral feast titles + octave-day titles; no scripture, nothing fetched. Mary's name rendered 马利亚 (CUV) per glossary; for Marian feast (Assumption) used 至圣童贞马利亚 per glossary §9. Many saints have no fixed CUV/Protestant form, so used established Catholic transliterations: Pius=庇护, Sixtus/Xystus=西斯笃, Peter Chanel=沙尼, Padre Pio=庇护神父, Charbel=沙尔贝, Edith Stein/Teresa Benedicta=圣十字德兰, Therese of Lisieux=婴孩耶稣的德兰, Teresa of Avila=圣德兰. Biblical-name saints use CUV: Peter=彼得 (but compound modern names like Petrus Canisius/Damiani/Celestini/Chrysologus kept Catholic 伯多禄 to match conventional usage of those non-biblical figures — flag if uniform 彼得 preferred), Stephen=司提反, Timothy=提摩太, Titus=提多, Thomas the Apostle=多马 (but Aquinas/Villanova=托马斯 per glossary). 'O. N.' (Ordinis Nostri)=本会. Archidiœcesis=总教区. Turibius of Mogrovejo=多里维. Some obscure transliterations (Nonnosus, Symmachus, Telesphorus, Silverius, Vitalianus, Zosimus) are my best renderings.
